title: rosa villosa subsp. omissa
intro: Rosa villosa subsp. omissa, also known as the hairy rose or fragrant wild rose, is a subspecies of flowering shrub that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing, reduce pain and swelling, and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Rosa villosa subsp. omissa include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and improve immune function. Herbal preparations of Rosa villosa subsp. omissa include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the plant's flowers, leaves, or roots in hot water or a solvent.
